description: Failure-signature → finding/issue index maintained by /lmv-autodev. Each failure_signature maps a regex over wkmigrate not_translatable warning messages to a known wkmigrate weakness. lmv_issue is the local lmv gh issue (filled in by Phase 1.5 step 5 once filed). upstream_issues are references to ghanse/wkmigrate (informational only — never auto-filed). last_tested records the wkmigrate ref the signature was last validated against.

last_session.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs, wrapped as SetVariable, FLAT input shape)
last_session.harvest_outcome: 0 not_translatable entries from W-1..W-6 signatures; corpus exhausted on this wkmigrate ref

math_on_params.description: Math expressions over pipeline().parameters.X requiring type coercion

deep_nesting.description: Pipeline DAGs with deep activity nesting; can hit PipelineAdapter recursion

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] Map @concat(a, b, ...) to Python f-string / str concatenation (sub-finding of wkmigrate#27)

last_tested.result: 34/34 resolved, 34/34 byte-match expected_python, 0 not_translatable, 0 W-1 cluster hits
last_tested.verified_via_journey: First lands in pr/27-1-expression-parser@be9e3a5 (47-fn Python registry includes concat as Tier-1)

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Add a PythonEmitter mapping for `concat` in the parsers/ module, producing either a string-concat chain or an f-string.

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] Resolve @pipeline().parameters.X in non-notebook activities (generalization of wkmigrate#55)

last_tested.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs, all wrapped as SetVariable)
last_tested.result: Not exercised — corpus uses literal expressions only, never @pipeline().parameters.X. Need a stratified corpus that injects parameter references in SetVariable/IfCondition/Copy contexts. See L-F4 / L-F5.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Generalize the #55 fix beyond notebook activities. SetVariable, IfCondition predicates, and Copy source paths all need pipeline().parameters.X → {{job.parameters.X}} substitution.

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] Type coercion for @add/@mul/@sub/@div/@mod on parameters (dbutils.widgets.get returns strings)

last_tested.result: Math literals resolve fully (34/34 byte-match), but the corpus contains no @pipeline().parameters.X references inside math contexts. Type-coercion path is unexercised. Need adversarial fixtures injecting parameter references.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Track the declared parameter type from the pipeline spec; when emitting a pipeline().parameters.X reference inside a math context, wrap with the appropriate coercion (int/float).

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] Support @activity('Lookup').output.firstRow.X chaining in downstream activities
upstream_issues: ghanse/wkmigrate#27 (Lookup adoption deferred to proposed wkmigrate#28)

last_tested.result: Not exercised — corpus has no multi-activity chains and never wraps in Lookup. wkmigrate's Lookup translator adoption is explicitly deferred per dev/pr-strategy-issue-27.md (proposed issue #28). Need a multi-activity corpus to probe.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Translate Lookup activities into a notebook cell that assigns its result to a task-value; downstream references resolve via dbutils.jobs.taskValues.get(taskKey='Lookup', key='firstRow').

failure_signatures.title: [safety] Cycle detection in PipelineAdapter recursive enrichment

last_tested.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (single-activity SetVariable wrapper, no Execute Pipeline)
last_tested.result: Not exercised — corpus has no Execute Pipeline activities or recursive structure. Need a corpus with self-referencing or circular pipelines to trigger the recursion path.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Thread a `visited: set[str]` through PipelineAdapter recursive calls; raise NotTranslatableWarning + UnsupportedValue on revisit.

failure_signatures.title: [observability] Emit structured failure_mode tags on NotTranslatableWarning payloads

last_tested.result: Not directly testable — this is a wkmigrate enhancement, not a failure signature. The 200-pair corpus produced 0 not_translatable warnings of any kind, so structured tags would have nothing to attach to. The enhancement is still valid for adversarial corpora that DO produce warnings.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Extend NotTranslatableWarning with a `failure_mode` field, one of: function_not_mapped, type_coercion_missing, nesting_depth_exceeded, parameter_reference_dropped, activity_output_chain_unresolved, unsupported_activity_type.

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] wkmigrate Lookup translator crashes on Expression-typed source.sql_reader_query (sub-finding of wkmigrate#27)
upstream_issues: ghanse/wkmigrate#27 (deferred Lookup adoption to proposed wkmigrate#28)

last_tested.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs wrapped in Lookup.source.sql_reader_query context)
last_tested.result: 200/200 RESOLVED, 0 placeholders, 0 errors. _resolve_source_query routes the raw query through get_literal_or_expression() with ExpressionContext.LOOKUP_QUERY and stores the emitted Python-code string on LookupActivity.source_query. The lookup notebook embeds it verbatim in .option('query', ...). Verified by /wkmigrate-autodev hot-swap of the lmv wkmigrate dependency to pr/27-3-translator-adoption.

previous_test.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs wrapped in Lookup.source.sql_reader_query context)
previous_test.result: 200/200 hard crashes with AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'replace'. wkmigrate's Lookup translator assumes the sql_reader_query is a plain string and calls .replace() on it directly; when the source provides {type: Expression, value: ...} the call crashes.
previous_test.raw_artifact: /tmp/lmv_sweep_alpha1_v2.json (cell key 'string,lookup_query' and similar across all 6 categories)

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: wkmigrate's Lookup translator (in alpha_1@969e74d) needs to handle the Expression-typed sql_reader_query case. Current code does something like `query.replace(...)` directly on the source dict; should instead use get_literal_or_expression() (the same shared utility pr/27-1 introduced for SetVariable) to resolve the expression OR pass through as a NotTranslatableWarning + UnsupportedValue if the resolution fails.

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] wkmigrate Copy translator silently falls to placeholder for Expression-typed source.sql_reader_query (sub-finding of wkmigrate#27)
upstream_issues: ghanse/wkmigrate#27 (deferred Copy adoption to proposed wkmigrate#28)

diagnosis_correction.actual_root_cause: lmv test fixture wrap_in_copy_query in src/lakeflow_migration_validator/synthetic/activity_context_wrapper.py was emitting a TabularTranslator column_mapping with sink: {name: 'tgt_col'} (no 'type' field). wkmigrate's _parse_dataset_mapping requires sink.type and correctly raises UnsupportedValue('Missing value for key type in sink dataset'), which propagates to the activity-level UnsupportedValue and is converted to a /UNSUPPORTED_ADF_ACTIVITY placeholder by normalize_translated_result. The (type: Copy) regex match was catching an lmv malformed-fixture failure, NOT a wkmigrate Expression-handling failure.
diagnosis_correction.lmv_fix: Added 'type': 'string' to the sink mapping in wrap_in_copy_query. After the fix, lmv sweep against the same alpha_1@969e74d ref shows 200/200 with 0 placeholders, 0 errors on copy_query.
diagnosis_correction.wkmigrate_action_required: None. wkmigrate's Copy translator behavior is correct given the malformed input.

last_tested.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs wrapped in Copy.source.sql_reader_query context with FIXED TabularTranslator column_mapping including sink.type)
last_tested.result: 0/200 placeholders, 0/200 errors. resolved_count=0 because the L-F17 walker doesn't yet extract Copy.source.sql_reader_query as an ExpressionPair (lmv-side enhancement, tracked separately).

previous_test.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs wrapped in Copy.source.sql_reader_query context with the BROKEN column_mapping that omits sink.type)
previous_test.result: 200/200 placeholder warnings — caused by the missing sink.type, NOT the source query expression. See diagnosis_correction.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Same as W-7 but for Copy.source.sql_reader_query: use get_literal_or_expression() to resolve the source query expression. Note that the Copy translator already accepts a column_mapping (TabularTranslator) — the regression here is purely the source.sql_reader_query field.

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] wkmigrate Copy translator drops source.sql_reader_query in _parse_sql_format_options (sub-finding of wkmigrate#27)
upstream_issues: ghanse/wkmigrate#27 (deferred Copy adoption to proposed wkmigrate#28)
failure_signatures.discovered_via: L-F19 dropped-expression-field detector (lmv PR #27, commit a8a6397)

diagnosis.root_cause: wkmigrate's parsers/dataset_parsers.py::_parse_sql_format_options builds CopyActivity.source_properties from a fixed key list (type, query_isolation_level, query_timeout_seconds, numPartitions, batchsize, sessionInitStatement, mode) that does NOT include sql_reader_query. The query — literal OR Expression — is silently lost the moment translate_copy_activity runs. The Copy preparer then generates a JDBC read with no query, which would either pull the entire table or omit the WHERE clause depending on downstream code.
diagnosis.evidence: lmv L-F19 detector emits dropped_expression_field warnings on every Copy activity whose source dict has sql_reader_query as an Expression. Verified end-to-end via wrap_in_copy_query → adf_to_snapshot → expression_coverage drops to 0.0 (measurable=True).
diagnosis.differs_from_w7: W-7 was the Lookup translator hard-crashing on Expression sql_reader_query. W-9 is the Copy translator silently dropping it (literal or Expression). Different code paths, different fixes.
diagnosis.differs_from_w8: W-8 was an lmv fixture bug (missing sink.type). W-9 is a real wkmigrate gap that the W-8 fixture fix unblocked us to see.

last_tested.corpus: manual probe via wrap_in_copy_query (also affects pr/27-3-translator-adoption)
last_tested.result: Copy with Expression sql_reader_query: 0 resolved_expressions, 1 dropped_expression_field warning, expression_coverage=0.0 (measurable=True). Same on pr/27-3 — the sql_format_options drop is unchanged on that branch.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Extend _parse_sql_format_options in wkmigrate/parsers/dataset_parsers.py to extract sql_reader_query from the source definition. For literal strings, store directly. For {type: Expression, value: ...} dicts, route through get_literal_or_expression() with an appropriate ExpressionContext (analogous to ExpressionContext.LOOKUP_QUERY introduced in pr/27-1). Then have the Copy preparer / code generator embed the resolved query in the JDBC read expression's WHERE clause.

failure_signatures.title: [expressions] wkmigrate ForEach translator silently falls to placeholder for arbitrary items expressions (sub-finding of wkmigrate#27)

diagnosis_correction.actual_root_cause: Two facts: (1) ADF ForEach.items must evaluate to an array. The corpus uses expressions like @toUpper('abc'), @add(1,2), @equals(1,1) — these are scalars, so wkmigrate correctly produces a placeholder rather than emitting nonsense. (2) The 33 'collection'-category corpus entries are MOSTLY transformations OF arrays (e.g. @length(createArray(1,2,3)) → int, @first(createArray('a','b')) → element, @empty(createArray()) → bool), not bare arrays. They are valid test fixtures for the length/first/last/empty functions but NOT valid ForEach items. The corpus contains zero bare @createArray(...) expressions.
diagnosis_correction.wkmigrate_status: Bare @createArray(...) IS handled correctly by alpha_1's existing _parse_for_each_items at lines 207-218. Verified directly: wrap_in_for_each("@createArray('a','b','c')") returns ForEachActivity(items_string='["a","b","c"]') with 1 resolved expression and is_placeholder=False. The pr/27-3-translator-adoption branch refines the items resolution further but the alpha_1 baseline is functional.
diagnosis_correction.corpus_followup: The L-F3 adversarial corpus should grow at least one bare @createArray(...) expression so the W-10 fix has a positive test that lights up. Tracked as a corpus-growth task, not a wkmigrate issue.
diagnosis_correction.real_remaining_ux_issue: When ForEach receives a non-array expression, the placeholder warning carries the generic '(type: ForEach) was substituted ... did not recognise the source ADF activity type' message. The type WAS recognised, the items WAS evaluated, the items just didn't return an array. A more specific NotTranslatableWarning with failure_mode='items_expression_not_array' would be more useful — folded into W-6 (structured failure_mode tags) as a P3 messaging improvement.
diagnosis_correction.wkmigrate_action_required: None for W-10 itself. W-6 (structured failure_mode tags) covers the messaging improvement separately.

last_tested.result: Corpus sweep: 200/200 placeholders (correct — the corpus has no bare arrays). Manual bare-array probe: ForEachActivity(items_string='["a","b","c"]'), 1 resolved expression, is_placeholder=False (correct — alpha_1's createArray path works).

previous_test.corpus: golden_sets/expressions.json (200 pairs wrapped in ForEach.items context with non-empty inner activities array)
previous_test.result: 200/200 placeholder warnings — interpreted at the time as a wkmigrate bug, but see diagnosis_correction: it's a corpus-mismatch artifact.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: wkmigrate's ForEach translator should use get_literal_or_expression() for the items property and emit either a resolved Python list (when the expression statically evaluates to a literal array) or a runtime expression that produces an array. The pr/27-3-translator-adoption work covered the items field on the IR side but the placeholder fallback indicates the preparer never got the integration.

failure_signatures.regex: (?i)was substituted with a placeholder.*did not recognise
failure_signatures.title: [activity_coverage] Activity became placeholder — wkmigrate did not recognise the source ADF activity structure
failure_signatures.discovered_via: adversarial loop Campaign A (2026-04-11): all 60 pipelines hit this

diagnosis.root_cause: The adversarial generator creates Copy/ForEach/WebActivity/Lookup activities with expression-heavy properties but without the full structural elements wkmigrate requires (dataset definitions for Copy, linked service refs, etc.). wkmigrate correctly rejects these as unrecognized and substitutes placeholders. This is NOT an expression handling bug — it is a structural gap in how the generator constructs activities.
diagnosis.action: Either (a) improve the adversarial generator to produce structurally complete activities, or (b) accept that activity_coverage will be low for LLM-generated pipelines and focus adversarial testing on expression-level sweeps instead.

failure_signatures.fix_sketch: Two-pronged: (1) Teach the adversarial generator to produce structurally valid Copy/Lookup/ForEach activities with proper dataset definitions. (2) In wkmigrate, improve error messages to distinguish 'activity type not supported' from 'activity structure incomplete'.

notes: lmv_issue is null until /lmv-autodev Phase 1.5 step 5 files the issue.
upstream_issues are informational. /lmv-autodev does NOT auto-file in ghanse/wkmigrate.
When a new failure cluster is harvested in Phase 1.5 and matches no signature, /lmv-autodev appends a new entry here (and commits) before drafting the lmv issue.
Most regex patterns are matched against the `message` field of `not_translatable` entries from the lmv adapter (`match_target: 'not_translatable.message'`, the default).

Some signatures (e.g. W-7) match against captured exception strings instead — see `match_target: 'exception'`. The current /lmv-autodev Phase 1.5 matcher only walks not_translatable entries; the exception channel comes from `lmv sweep-activity-contexts` and requires extending the matcher OR enriching wkmigrate's NotTranslatableWarning emission to convert these crashes into structured warnings (W-6 territory).

L-F18 (PR #21): placeholder warnings emitted by lmv's adapter now include the original ADF activity type both as a structured `original_activity_type` field AND embedded in the message text as `(type: <Type>)`. W-8 and W-10 regexes were updated to match `(?i)\(type:\s*Copy\)` / `(?i)\(type:\s*ForEach\)` instead of relying on a brittle task_key substring (e.g. 'copy', 'foreach'). Older signatures that match by task_key remain valid as a fallback.
